title: Strategy context brief: PM superpower recovery
context_summary: ProductOS needs a sharper strategy spine before expanding discovery depth so the next-version work improves PM judgment rather than just adding more research outputs.

enterprise_goal_links.linkage_summary: The next version should make ProductOS feel strategically coherent enough that a buyer can understand the product bet quickly.
enterprise_goal_links.success_signal: Shorter time from mission intake to strategy-ready discovery packet.

portfolio_bets.rationale: The repo is already strong on governed execution, so the highest-leverage next bet is better framing and strategy quality upstream.
Capturing strategic context in-repo compounds discovery quality without broadening external claims.

business_model_outcomes.linkage_summary: A clearer strategy spine should make ProductOS easier to explain, evaluate, and trust.
The product should claim sharper PM judgment, not broader autonomy than the repo can prove.

strategic_constraints.constraint: Keep the stable line and external claim boundary unchanged.
strategic_constraints.consequence: The slice must stay internal-first and should not depend on a new external product surface.

strategic_constraints.constraint: Favor repo-native, reviewable standards over prompt-only behavior.
strategic_constraints.consequence: Strategy logic needs explicit contracts, workflow rules, and self-hosting artifacts.

open_questions: How much of the next customer buying signal depends on strategy clarity versus execution proof?

evidence_refs.justification: The mission defines the PM-facing problem and business goal for the next slice.
Market dynamics show that trusted judgment and traceability matter more than generic drafting acceleration alone.
